response.justification: Bogdan is a strong, versatile engineer with genuine craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and rare directly-relevant experience building developer tools — he wrote C/C++ components for the LabWindows/CVI IDE and authored language-tooling libraries (a test runner and assertion library in D). However, the role's core required stack is a poor match: his depth is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, with Java appearing only peripherally, no Kotlin, no Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate, and no demonstrated deep JVM expertise. The IDE/tooling affinity makes him interesting, but the enterprise-JVM gap is significant for a senior IC expected to ship Spring/Kotlin features end-to-end.
strengths: Direct IDE and language-tooling experience (LabWindows/CVI IDE, custom test runner and assertion libraries)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, pairing, CI/CD, and high-performance/low-latency systems work
Broad, proven track record across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded with large-scale codebases
concerns: No Kotlin and no Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate experience, which are core required qualifications
Java appears only as a secondary skill; no evidence of deep JVM internals knowledge
Primary expertise (TypeScript/Node.js, DLang) sits outside the enterprise-JVM focus of the role
